Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Tony Hoare has died 1888 upvotes, 247 comments
  2. Standardizing source maps 61 upvotes, 5 comments
  3. Julia Snail – An Emacs Development Environment for Julia Like Clojure's Cider 117 upvotes, 15 comments
  4. U+237C ⍼ Is Azimuth 358 upvotes, 60 comments
  5. Whistleblower: DOGE member took Social Security data to new job 111 upvotes, 27 comments
  6. TADA: Fast, Reliable Speech Generation Through Text-Acoustic Synchronization 74 upvotes, 19 comments
  7. Universal vaccine against respiratory infections and allergens 309 upvotes, 114 comments
  8. UK MPs give ministers powers to restrict Internet for under 18s 13 upvotes, 1 comments
  9. Building a TB-303 from Scratch 142 upvotes, 48 comments
  10. Cloudflare crawl endpoint 378 upvotes, 148 comments
  11. PeppyOS: A simpler alternative to ROS 2 (now with containers support) 31 upvotes, 7 comments
  12. The entities enabling scientific fraud at scale are large, resilient and growing 24 upvotes, 5 comments
  13. Surpassing vLLM with a Generated Inference Stack 52 upvotes, 16 comments
  14. SSH Secret Menu 276 upvotes, 126 comments
  15. Levels of Agentic Engineering 230 upvotes, 106 comments
  16. Zig – Type Resolution Redesign and Language Changes 326 upvotes, 159 comments
  17. AI Agent Hacks McKinsey 46 upvotes, 16 comments
  18. Writing my own text editor, and daily-driving it 152 upvotes, 70 comments
  19. Lego's 0.002 mm Specification and Its Implications for Manufacturing (2025) 71 upvotes, 34 comments
  20. When the chain becomes the product: Seven years inside a token-funded venture 32 upvotes, 12 comments
  21. Launch HN: RunAnywhere (YC W26) – Faster AI Inference on Apple Silicon 228 upvotes, 134 comments
  22. Microsoft BitNet: 100B Param 1-Bit model for local CPUs 108 upvotes, 63 comments
  23. Create value for others and don’t worry about the returns 514 upvotes, 363 comments
  24. WA income tax clears House after 24-hour debate 26 upvotes, 11 comments
  25. Debian decides not to decide on AI-generated contributions 353 upvotes, 266 comments
  26. Yann LeCun raises $1B to build AI that understands the physical world 532 upvotes, 442 comments
  27. Show HN: Custom Git-receive-pack implementation 7 upvotes, 2 comments
  28. RISC-V Is Sloooow 278 upvotes, 293 comments
  29. Agents that run while I sleep 376 upvotes, 425 comments
  30. Roblox is minting teen millionaires 188 upvotes, 229 comments

Last generated 10:30pm, 11 March 2026. Source here.
